message:
fix: canonicalize STORAGE_ROOT at startup to support symlinked paths (#73)

When STORAGE_ROOT is a symlink (common deployment pattern), the
aggressive symlink-rejection guards in ensure_dir() and
atomic_write_bytes() would reject the path, causing archive writes
to silently fail in the Write-Behind Queue after 3 retries.

The fix adds a canonicalize_storage_root() helper that resolves the
storage root path to its real (symlink-free) canonical form during
Config::from_env(). This handles two cases:

1. Path already exists: std::fs::canonicalize resolves all symlinks
2. Path doesn't exist yet (first run): walks upward to find the
   deepest existing ancestor, canonicalizes that prefix, then
   re-appends the not-yet-created tail components

After canonicalization, all downstream symlink checks pass because
the resolved path contains no symlink components. The symlink guards
continue to protect against symlink escape attacks within the
resolved tree.

Failure to canonicalize is non-fatal — the original path is kept and
later I/O will surface any real filesystem problems.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
